meaning: n.骑马侍从,先驱者,摩托车警卫
examples: Posing as a bold outrider, as he did at the summit, may yet help.
In the space battle that ensued over the Imperial capital, Dash's Outrider appeared to sustain critical damage from exploding space debris.
He was finally brought to a halt with the help of an outrider.
Earlier this month, Nick Boles, a new Tory MP and kite-flying outrider for the modernising camp, called for the two parties to forge an electoral pact at the next election.
